response.justification: The candidate is primarily a web and TypeScript engineer whose C++ exposure is confined to a single historical role writing components for NI LabWindows and a university thesis project; C++ does not even appear in their skills list. There is no evidence of expertise in industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle practices, multi-threaded systems design at scale, or industrial automation domain knowledge — all of which are core requirements for this role. The candidate's recent trajectory moves further from C++ and embedded systems, not toward them.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven architecture experience through a self-founded geo-data platform and low-latency async I/O work
Strong software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code review culture) transferable to any engineering context
Foundational C/C++ exposure via the NI LabWindows role and a C++ wave field synthesis thesis project
concerns: C++ is absent from the candidate's explicit skills list; current primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies
No evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, industrial communication protocols, or IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards
No demonstrated experience with networked multi-threaded systems design, message-broker architectures, or cloud industrial platforms such as Azure IoT
